title: nestotus lanuginosus var. andersonii
intro: Nestotus lanuginosus var. andersonii, also known as Desert Four O'Clock or Mirabilis andersonii, is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of the plant include treating various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
